events.summary: Goldman Sachs has stated that the United Arab Emirates' decision to exit OPEC poses a greater upside risk to oil supply in the medium term rather than the short term. The bank believes that while the current closure of the Strait limits UAE output, the exit could lead to an increase in crude production once Gulf export routes reopen, potentially reaching over 4.5 million barrels per day by February 2026. This move also implies an upside risk to Goldman's base case for UAE crude production recovery to 3.8 million bpd by October 2026, compared to 3.6 million bpd before the recent conflict.
events.title: Goldman says UAE's exit from OPEC raises medium-term oil supply upside risk

However, its Price-to-Earnings (P/E) ratio of 20.28x is below industry and peer averages, and Simply Wall St's "Fair Ratio" of 24.99x, suggesting it might be undervalued based on earnings multiples. events.summary: South Korea will soon no longer be one of the few countries where Google Maps doesn't work properly, after its security-conscious government reversed a two-decade stance to approve ‌the export of high-precision map data to overseas servers.  Those conditions include blurring military and other sensitive security-related facilities, as well as ​restricting longitude and latitude coordinates for South Korean territory on products such as Google Maps and Google Earth, it said.  The decision is expected to hurt Naver and Kakao - local internet giants which currently dominate the country's market for digital map services.
